subject: VectorLibrary — hub-hosted decision pattern and approach knowledge base (fourth memory graph)
title: VectorLibrary — Hub Knowledge Vectors with Tool Gate and Adoption Tracking
what: Defines VectorLibrary as the fourth intelligence graph (PathGraph + TasteGraph + ToolGraph + VectorLibrary). VectorLibrary is a hub-hosted catalogue of reusable decision patterns, validated approaches, and solved problems — each entry is a vector (a directional solution). Active vectors carry requires_tools[] (what a spoke needs to adopt this vector) and adopted_by[] (which spokes have adopted, at what version). Retired vectors record retirement_reason and retirement_date. Vector adoption is gated by ToolGraph: the spoke's access_matrix must contain all requires_tools before adoption is recorded. Spokes never write to the hub library — they propose vectors via lug delivery; the hub Gardener reviews and promotes.
why: Currently each spoke rediscovers solutions independently. A teaching propagates behavioral rules but not validated decision patterns (the why behind choosing approach X over Y). VectorLibrary closes the knowledge-reuse gap: a pattern proven in one spoke becomes available to all spokes that have the required tooling. The adoption gate prevents incompatible adoption — a spoke cannot accidentally adopt a Supabase-dependent pattern when it has no Supabase access. Retirement propagation ensures stale patterns are pulled before they cause harm.
audience: All agents making architectural or approach decisions — check VectorLibrary before designing from scratch
Navigator — surfaces relevant active vectors during session portfolio planning
Hub Gardener — curates the library: promotion, versioning, retirement
Sentinel — monitors health of approaches adopted from VectorLibrary
Any agent at closeout — consider whether a novel solution warrants authoring a new vector
use_cases: Pattern reuse: spoke solves drift-detection in a novel way; at closeout it authors a vector proposal lug (routed_to=HUB); Gardener promotes it; other spokes can adopt
Navigator recommends: during session planning, Navigator surfaces 2 active vectors relevant to the current work goal alongside lug recommendations
Adoption gate: spoke wants vector with requires_tools=[supabase-rest, ollama-local]; ToolGraph access_matrix missing ollama-local — adoption blocked with clear reason
Retirement: a vector based on a deprecated API retires with reason='wai-sync-v1 removed in v2.1' and retirement_date; adopting spokes receive a teaching
Fleet adoption view: hub sees 12/30 spokes adopted vector-session-guard-pattern; flags remaining 18 for outreach teaching
patterns: Hub-hosted, spoke-read: vectors live at hub/WAI-Hub/vectors/active/ and hub/WAI-Hub/vectors/retired/ — spokes read, never write directly
Spoke adoption record: WAI-Spoke/adopted-vectors.json tracks which vectors this spoke has adopted and at what version
requires_tools gate: must pass ToolGraph.access_matrix check before adoption is recorded in adopted-vectors.json
Retirement propagation: hub retires a vector → teaching published → spokes update adopted-vectors.json with retirement_note
Vector versioning: vectors carry semver; adopted_by[] records version at adoption time — enables drift detection when vector updates
Authoring pipeline: spoke proposes (task lug routed_to=HUB) → Gardener reviews → promoted to active library

vector_entry.title: string
vector_entry.summary: string — one paragraph: the problem this solves, the approach taken, when to apply it
requires_tools: tool_id — from ToolGraph schema; spoke must have all of these in access_matrix
vector_entry.authored_by: wheel_id — spoke that originated this vector

adopted.retirement_note: string | null — set when the adopted vector is later retired

constraints: Vectors live at the hub — spokes NEVER write vector entries directly to hub/WAI-Hub/vectors/
To propose a new vector: spoke creates a task lug with routed_to=HUB; lug body must include a draft vector_entry JSON for Gardener to review
requires_tools entries must reference valid tool_ids from the ToolGraph schema — no free-text tool names
adopted_by[] is maintained by the hub Gardener on nightly sync — spokes do not self-update this field in the hub file
A retired vector with non-empty adopted_by[] triggers a teaching to notify all adopting spokes of the retirement
Vector retirement is permanent: no un-retiring; if the approach is revived, create a new vector with supersedes referencing the old one
Spokes update adopted-vectors.json locally when they adopt a vector or when a retirement teaching arrives — this is a spoke-maintained file
tests: Adoption gate blocked: spoke access_matrix missing a required tool; adoption attempt returns clear error with the missing tool_id
Adoption gate pass: all requires_tools present in spoke access_matrix; adopted-vectors.json updated with vector_id, version_adopted, and adopted_at
Retirement propagation: vector retired in hub library; teaching published; spoke processes teaching and adds retirement_note to its adopted-vectors.json entry
Library index integrity: all files in hub/WAI-Hub/vectors/active/ appear in index.json; count matches active_count
Supersession flow: vector V1 retired with superseded_by=V2; adopters of V1 receive adoption prompt for V2 in next teaching batch
Proposal lug review: task lug routed_to=HUB with draft vector_entry; Gardener promotes to active/ and updates index.json

open_questions: Navigator surfacing mechanism: semantic search on summary+tags, or tag-based exact matching? Semantic requires embedding infra.
adopted-vectors.json: committed or gitignored? It reflects spoke state (not framework code) and changes frequently.
First seed vector: what existing proven pattern in this spoke is the best candidate for the first vector entry?

perceive: Read this spec fully. Check if hub/WAI-Hub/vectors/ directory exists. Read spec-toolgraph-v1 for the access_matrix gate implementation. Check WAI-Spoke/adopted-vectors.json if it exists. Read WAI-Spoke/advisors/navigator/recommendations-current.json to understand current Navigator behavior.
execute: 1. Create hub/WAI-Hub/vectors/active/ and hub/WAI-Hub/vectors/retired/ directories. 2. Create hub/WAI-Hub/vectors/index.json scaffold with active_count=0, retired_count=0, vectors=[]. 3. Create WAI-Spoke/adopted-vectors.json scaffold for this spoke. 4. Implement ToolGraph adoption gate in vector adoption workflow: check spoke ToolGraph.json access_matrix before recording adoption. 5. Add VectorLibrary sync step to Gardener nightly: aggregate adopted_by[] from all spokes, detect retirements with non-empty adopted_by. 6. Author first vector entry from an existing proven pattern in this spoke (e.g. session-guard pattern).
verify: 1. hub/WAI-Hub/vectors/index.json exists and parses cleanly. 2. WAI-Spoke/adopted-vectors.json exists and parses cleanly. 3. Attempt adoption of vector with missing required tool in spoke access_matrix — blocked with tool_id in error. 4. Adoption with all tools present — adopted-vectors.json updated correctly. 5. Retire a test vector; retirement_note propagates to adopting spoke record.
